description: §60.5/§60.9 — an endpoint whose resolved `: ResponseT` is a non-variant type (here a struct `User`) fires the info-lint W-API-RESPONSE-NOT-VARIANT (typer, W4). parseVariant (§41.13) is variant-only, so the response is raw-passed and the §60.5 boundary-parse guarantee does not apply; the lint keeps that raw boundary explicit (§60.3). The code is severity `info` — it lands in the warnings partition, never the errors stream.
rationale: §60.5: a non-variant `ResponseT` cannot be parseVariant-decoded and is raw-passed; the typer fires W-API-RESPONSE-NOT-VARIANT (Info, §34) so the raw boundary is explicit at the declaration site. severity:info asserts the §34 partition — it is NOT an Error (the cross-stream-honest check).
